title: Where on the Site?
objective: Survive a 3-minute run by replaying increasingly dense pulse sequences across room tabs, building combo while adapting to rule shifts that change where the sequence must be sent.

controls.outcome: Sends the memorized signal to the correct place and keeps combo alive

controls.input: Watch the center banner and audio cue, then tap forward, reverse, or muted-skip
controls.outcome: Applies the current 'where' condition for bonus scoring

controls.input: Tap the flashing safe tab before the beat expires
controls.outcome: Prevents an immediate page crash but breaks multiplier

steps: Four room tabs sit on a phone-sized 'site' map: Lobby, Board, Sketch, and Match.
A short pulse pattern plays as light, vibration, and pitch steps hopping between tabs.
The player replays the exact route by tapping the tabs in order before a fast timeout ring closes.
Every few rounds, a 'Where Shift' changes the rule for the next pattern: replay backward, ignore dim pulses, or send only pulses that landed
Correct replays add one pulse to the next pattern, increase tempo, and raise the combo multiplier.
A wrong tap, late tap, or forbidden tap costs one of three signal bars; losing all bars crashes the page and ends the run.
scoring: Score comes from correct taps plus end-of-pattern bonuses. Consecutive perfect patterns raise a multiplier up to x8. Clearing a pattern during a Where Shift grants a punchy 'Hot Route' bonus. Local best score and fastest
tips: Listen first: each tab has its own tone, so audio helps more than color alone.
During reverse rounds, track the final pulse location because it becomes your first tap.
If a tab dims, it is muted for skip rounds; tapping it breaks combo instantly.
Short perfect streaks matter more than greedy recovery because multiplier drives leaderboard score.

trendContext.title: I built a browser site where you can play classic multiplayer games with friends (Bingo, Draw & Guess, Chess, Tic Tac Toe) — would love feedback
